title: sabulina urumiensis
intro: Sabulina urumiensis, also known as the "Urumi-kaikadi" or "Indian sandwort," is a small, herbaceous plant native to the Western Ghats in India. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. In traditional Indian medicine, Sabulina urumiensis is often prepared as a decoction or a powder and used in herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, or topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
